config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once individual rights are treated as secondary, it becomes too easy for governments or majorities to justify abuse. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting basic rights first, then balancing them with the common good when there is a real and limited need. From my own perspective, I am wary of arguments that sound collective but end up silencing people who are already vulnerable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sekadar prioritas yang bisa dikalahkan kapan saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya sangat curiga pada argumen yang menempatkan “kepentingan bersama” di atas hak individu, karena dalam sejarah kami itu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan diskriminasi, sensor, atau penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh mayoritas. Tentu ada keadaan darurat di mana masyarakat perlu membatasi kebebasan tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, frasa “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi alasan untuk mengorbankan orang yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should have blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “for the public good” can be used to pressure people to stay quiet, especially when the state or the majority decides whose voice matters less. Social harmony is important in our culture, but harmony without firm individual rights can become conformity enforced by power. The better principle is that society and individual rights must be balanced, with rights as the baseline and any limits tightly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Hak individu tetap harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sesuatu yang bisa dikalahkan hanya karena mayoritas mengaku mewakili “kepentingan masyarakat.” Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, saya tidak percaya pada gagasan bahwa kebaikan bersama otomatis lebih sah daripada hak orang per orang, karena sejarah kami berkali-kali menunjukkan bagaimana alasan itu dipakai untuk membungkam minoritas, membatasi kebebasan berbicara, atau menormalisasi diskriminasi. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar tidak ditempatkan sebagai garis merah, maka “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi individu terlebih dahulu, lalu membatasi kebebasan hanya ketika ada kebutuhan yang benar-benar jelas, sempit, dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of social harmony, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence dissent, pressure minorities, and excuse unfair treatment. The strongest point in the discussion is that limited restrictions can be justified in real emergencies, but those limits must be narrow and accountable, not a general rule. For me, individual rights are the baseline, because once they are treated as optional, the people with less power are the first to suffer.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Hak individu tidak boleh ditempatkan di bawah kepentingan masyarakat secara umum, karena bagi saya itu terlalu berbahaya dan terlalu mudah disalahgunakan. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat jelas bagaimana dalih “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, dan dominasi mayoritas atas minoritas. Kebutuhan kolektif memang ada, tetapi kalau hak dasar tidak menjadi garis batas utama, maka yang paling lemah justru akan menanggung biayanya. Jadi, saya menilai masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang otomatis memprioritaskan kolektif, melainkan yang membatasi kekuasaan demi melindungi individu terlebih dahulu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
